goal: Design per-row supervised live-proof runs for the 63 needs-live-proof input/cursor rows: proof architecture, per-row plans, fallback requirements, env-gated harness, and receipt-gated promotion.

tooDangerous: cursor.invisible - the preview value itself removes cursor visibility

passedProof.fallbackUsed: keyboard and pointer both remained fully usable; only idle cursor-hide timing changed; the revert needed no user input

fallbackStrategies: cursor rows: keyboard and pointer events keep flowing; only cursor appearance/warp behavior changes
keyboard rows: pointer remains usable to click Revert/Cancel
touchpad/tablet/touch rows: external mice and keyboards are unaffected
focus rows: both devices keep working; keyboard navigation remains available
all rows: the dead-man timeout auto-revert fires without any user input
worst case: TTY (Ctrl+Alt+F3) rollback instruction with the recorded original value, or restart Hyprland; the config file is never modified
recoveryInstructions: every plan carries a row-specific recovery instruction combining the automatic countdown revert with the TTY rollback path
sourceGuards: the input proof module contains no command construction, no config writes, no reload path, and no active config references
the live harness is ignored by default and requires HYPRLAND_SETTINGS_RUN_INPUT_LIVE_PROOF=1 plus an explicit row id
the harness gate refuses unknown rows, non-input rows, rows without fallback, non-proof-ready rows, and already-proven rows
rows are promoted only via PROVEN_INPUT_ROWS receipts; the dead-man layer arms input/cursor rows only when a receipt exists (test-enforced)
UI proof copy is display-only: no hyprctl strings, no executor calls, no gate access from UI code
testsAdded: per_row_input_live_proof_applies_and_reverts (ignored, env-gated; run once for cursor.inactive_timeout: passed)
nextRecommendedWork: Run further per-row env-gated live proofs one at a time, starting with the safest remaining cursor rows (hide_on_key_press, no_warps, hotspot_padding), promoting each only on a passed receipt; the structured-family active-config pilot still awaits its explicit user decision.
